text: Thanks for joining us today.
In this session, we'll be asking you to complete a task on a real website while sharing your thoughts out loud.
An AI researcher will guide you through the process and ask you some questions along the way.
There are no right or wrong answers, we're interested in your honest experience.
Ready to get started?
Yep.
To start, can you tell me about what you typically look for when you're choosing domestic flights?
Cheapest price is usually what I look for, and then also directness of flight.
When you say directness of flight, could you explain what you mean by that?
Yeah, I don't really want to stop off in another city.
I want to go direct from my home state to the destination.
Can you tell me about a time recently when you booked a domestic flight and prioritized the cheapest price and how that decision process unfolded?
Yeah, the latest one, I basically just— the way I do the cheapest price is just to vary the dates to get the cheapest price.
And that was the last trip I booked to Queensland coming up in June.
How did you go about comparing prices across different platforms for that trip to Queensland?
Yeah, I usually do Google Flights.
And after checking Google Flights, what was your next step in the booking process for that Queensland trip?
Yeah, okay, so I go to Google Flights, I can see which is the cheapest airline, and then I just literally go direct to the airline then and book it direct with them.
And why do you prefer to book directly with the airline rather than through a third-party site after finding a good price on Google Flights?
[Speaker:JAMES] Good question.
Yeah, I just always like to use the airline website over a third party.
I'm not sure why, but maybe cancellation, that type of stuff.
[Speaker:AI RESEARCHER] You mentioned cancellation, that type of stuff.
Can you elaborate on what concerns you have about cancellations when When booking through a third party versus directly with the airline?
Booking flights with Flight Centre, Webjet, if you need to change them, then the refund sits within a credit within Webjet.
It's not as hard to do.
Sorry, it's not as easy to do.
I just like to do it directly with the airline rather than a third party.
And usually I'm not getting any cheaper flights anyway.
Could you describe a specific instance where you had to change a flight booked through a third party and how that experience differed from changing a flight booked directly with an airline?
[Speaker:JAMES] I can't think of an example myself.
I've just heard people talk about other issues they've had where it's been way harder than they thought.

[Speaker:AI RESEARCHER] When you've heard about these difficulties, what specifically did you understand to be the main problem or challenge people faced when trying to change a flight booked through a third party?
[Speaker:JAMES] Getting onto them 24/7 for a start, whereas an airline you can, and you can go up to them in the airport as well.

[Speaker:RESEARCHER] So if I understand correctly, the perceived difficulty in reaching customer service or getting in-person support from third-party booking sites is a major factor in your preference for direct airline bookings.
Is that right?
Yes.
Thanks for sharing your insights so far.
Next, we're going to begin a website usability test.
Please share your screen, and I'll provide you with some instructions for your task.
Then you can open the website in your browser.
Please click the screen sharing button that appears and choose the option to share this window.
Yep.
It looks like your screen share is working.
Here is your task.
Imagine you need to book a return domestic flight within Australia for an upcoming trip.
Using the Flight Centre website, search for flights, select your preferred options, and proceed through the booking flow, including reviewing any ancillaries and seat selection.
Please think out loud as you go.
Share what you're looking at, what you're thinking, and any reactions you have.
You can stop once you reach the page asking for traveler details.
You don't need to enter any personal information or complete the actual purchase.
I'm going to open the Flight Centre website now.
Please tell me what you are looking at and thinking as you complete the task.
Yes, so I'm looking at the home page.
I can see deals.
I can see my city.
I'm going to choose where I fly to.
I'm going to fly to Melbourne, and I'm going to see that there are cheap flights this weekend, which is later in March.
I'm going to go Friday to Sunday, seems like the cheapest way to do it.
I'm going to get rid of that.
I'm going to search flights now.
It's doing comparisons, right?
So I can see that Virgin is the cheap— or Jetstar.
I just don't want to go Jetstar.
The difference between Jetstar and Virgin is only $20, so I'm going to click the light Virgin, and then Coming back, it's a bit of a—
What's making you hesitate?
Sorry, I was just looking.
Yeah, Virgin.
Jetstar is a bit cheaper coming back.
I'm going to go with Virgin because it's only $20 more in light.
There we go.
That is doing the selection review trip.
So $333 return, it's not too bad considering it's leaving on Friday morning and coming back Sunday.
It doesn't give me any baggage, that's fine.
Doesn't let me choose seats.
I don't need put any more.
It's confirming flights.
Yep, so there we go, we're done.
I don't want to put any details in, so I'm coming back to you now.
Thank you for working through that.
Shall we move on to the next part?
Yeah.
What happened?
We just went through the process of selecting a flight.
Can you tell me about your experience with the seat selection part of that process?
Yeah, I didn't bother.
Honestly, I'm not paying extra for seats.
I don't care.
Like, there's one person that's not going to not give me a seat.
I don't really care.
You mentioned you didn't bother with seat selection.
Can you tell me more about why you made that choice?
[Speaker:JAMES] I don't want to pay for it, and I don't care where I sit.
[Speaker:AI] You've clearly stated you don't want to pay extra and don't care where you sit.
Can you tell me what goes through your mind when you see those additional charges for seat selection during the booking process?
[Speaker:JAMES] Yeah, it's just a waste of money.
I'm used to flying with Ryanair in Europe, where literally who's first on the plane gets to choose the seat.
Sit anywhere.
I'm used to that.
I really couldn't care less where I sit for an hour.
Same as getting a bus to work that takes 45 minutes.
What's the difference?
I'm not paying for my seat on the bus.
I'm not paying for it on a flight.
That's a really clear comparison.
So when you see other airlines or even this booking flow present options to pay for specific seats, How does that experience compare to your expectation that a seat is just included, like on a bus?
It is included, like on a bus.
I'm just not paying to sit because I have no idea who I'm sitting next to when I pay my money.
If it tells me who I'm sitting next to, that's an interesting way of looking at it, but it's never going to do that.
It's never going to say that the seat's empty because it never gets updated until right before the last minute anyway.
And if you go to a check in person at the airport, sometimes they can wangle you a specific seat, not that I would anyway.
So I just think paying for seats is a complete waste of money.
You've given some great reasons for not paying for seats, and you also mentioned that checking in at the airport might offer a chance for a specific seat, though you wouldn't bother.
Can you walk me through your typical process for getting a seat assignment on a flight when you choose not to pay for it?
Lucky dip, I couldn't care less.
I'll just, when I go to check in, it just gives me a seat and that's where I'm sitting.
So it sounds like you're perfectly content with a lucky dip approach to seating.
Has there ever been a time when the assigned seat you received without paying caused any frustration or inconvenience during your flight?
No, and I've never not paid for a seat and had to stand on an aeroplane.
Take that however you want.
That's a very direct way of putting it.
So you've never had a negative experience from not paying for a seat.

Thinking about the overall booking experience on Flight Centre, from when you first searched for flights to the point where you saw the seat selection options, what were your general impressions of the process itself?
All right.
It just looked like your bog-standard plug-and-play website across a lot of different platforms.
There was nothing really anything different from Flight Centre than any other website.
It all looked like it was the same plugin.
Yeah, you said it looked like bog-standard plug-and-play and similar to other platforms.
Can you describe what specifically gave you that impression?
Yeah, I've just seen that screen lots of times on other sites.
I can't remember exact specific ones, but It looked like I'd used it a million times before.
You mentioned seeing the screen many times before on other sites.
When you encounter a booking flow that feels so familiar, does that familiarity make the process easier or more difficult for you?
Easier.
That makes sense.

Since familiarity contributes to an easy experience, can you recall any specific elements or features on the Flight Centre booking pages that stood out to you as particularly well-designed or intuitive during the process?
[Speaker:JAMES] Not really.
It was all just pretty easy and familiar.
Nothing stood out.
[Speaker:RESEARCHER] So the overall impression was one of seamless familiarity, with nothing specifically standing out as exceptionally good or bad.
If you could change or improve one thing about the entire Flight Centre booking experience you just went through, What would it be?
I don't know if there's anything needed to be changed.
It's just because I thought it was bog-standard doesn't mean it's bad.
I thought it was all right, and there's nothing that stood out that was terrible.
It was fine.
Fine sometimes is good.
That's a very fair point.
Fine sometimes is good.
It sounds like the process met your expectations for a standard flight booking even if it didn't offer any groundbreaking new features.
Thank you so much for sharing all your thoughts and insights throughout this session.
Your feedback is really valuable to us.
